selected_important_turn.text: Well, I tell you what, I'd be willing to take 2 packages of water and 2 of firewood and leave the rest to you, including all of the food. How does that sound?🙂
selected_important_turn.why_important: This counter-offer is the clearest turning point: it shifts the exchange into competitive, zero-sum bargaining by responding to the opponent's demand with an assertive, self-favoring allocation. That escalation determines tone and makes subsequent acceptance or concession hinge on this split.
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: It's unnecessarily demanding (claims extra firewood in addition to 2 waters) and doesn't acknowledge the opponent's strong water need; it closes off integrative possibilities and risks damaging fairness perceptions and trust. Although it worked here, the move risks unnecessary conflict in many cases.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Soften the counter by recognizing the opponent's high water need and offer a trade that balances both sides (e.g., propose a specific split that gives them more water while conceding something you value less) or present multiple options (a fair compromise and a slightly selfish alternative) instead of a one-sided take-all allocation.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase (avoids zero-sum framing and opens room for mutually acceptable creative trades)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase (a balanced counter feels more equitable and reduces perceived greed)
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase (more likely to be accepted quickly if it acknowledges the other's priority)

value2reason.Low: I have enough food to get by. More would be nice, but it's a luxury.
value2reason.Medium: It gets cool at night and I want a big fire, but I don't want to spend time collecting it.
value2reason.High: I like hiking and need more water to stay hydrated while active.

value2reason.Low: We need additional package of this item to sustain us and give us nourishment and maintain our well being.
value2reason.Medium: Actually this is needed to make fire for food and most importantly to warm ourselves during cold weathers
value2reason.High: The main reason we need additional packages of this item is because it sustains even wen all other constraints are unavailable

dialogue.text: hello!
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Hello.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Can i kindly know your preferences please?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, I'm kind of low on water, so I'd like to get as much of that as I can. I'd like to go hiking and I don't really have enough water for a long trip. What about you?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Ohh nice.I also have the same issue around here.The thirst for water is growing high than ever due to drought
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, that's a bummer. How are you set for food? While I wouldn't mind a bit more, I'm in pretty good shape there, so I'd be willing to trade it for water. I'd hate to leave without getting a good hike in.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: Hun,It's a bit of a fair deal,but due to the condition here i will need a bit more of water though foo cannot be overlooked also for survival but still to survive we take chances.So do you mind me giving you a package of food and u trading 2 pakages of water?.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Well, I tell you what, I'd be willing to take 2 packages of water and 2 of firewood and leave the rest to you, including all of the food. How does that sound?🙂
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand your need for water. How about this: I'll take 2 waters, 1 firewood, and 1 food. You can have the remaining 1 water, 2 firewoods, and 2 foods. It balances our priorities and ensures we both have enough for the trip.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate the offer, but I need more water. How about I take 2 waters, 2 firewoods, and 1 food, while you get 1 water, 1 firewood, and 2 foods? It better aligns with my priority for water.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your need for water. How about this: I'll take 2 waters, 1 firewood, and 2 foods. You can have 1 water, 2 firewoods, and 1 food. It gives you more water while I get more food, which aligns with our priorities.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need more water. Let's try 3 waters, 1 firewood, and 1 food for me, and 0 waters, 2 firewoods, and 2 foods for you. It prioritizes my high need for water while still giving you enough firewood and food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can't give up all the water. How about 2 waters, 2 firewoods, and 1 food for me, and 1 water, 1 firewood, and 2 foods for you? It's fair and meets both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I need at least 2 waters. How about 2 waters, 2 firewoods, and 1 food for me, and 1 water, 1 firewood, and 2 foods for you? It meets my priority for water while ensuring you get enough food.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I can't give up all the water. How about 2 waters, 2 firewoods, and 1 food for me, and 1 water, 1 firewood, and 2 foods for you? It's fair and meets both our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I'm okay with 2 waters, 2 firewoods, and 1 food for me, and 1 water, 1 firewood, and 2 foods for you. It meets my priority for water while ensuring you get enough food. Let's agree on this split.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
